fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer pharmacist license to tennessee, pharmacist compact tennessee, tennessee pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Tennessee Board of Pharmacy
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Tennessee.

steps.title: Create Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Create a user account on the Licensure and Regulatory System (LARS) if you do not have one. Log in, complete the 'Pharmacist Licensure by Endorsement' application, and pay the required fee through the LARS portal.

steps.title: Provide NABP e-Profile ID
steps.description: Ensure your NABP e-Profile ID is provided during the LARS application process, as this is essential for transferring your licensure information.

steps.title: Request NABP Licensure Transfer
steps.description: Request a Pharmacist Licensure Transfer Application through the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to be sent directly to the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy. This includes verification of your original licensure and all other licenses held.

steps.title: Register for and Pass TN MPJE
steps.description: Once eligibility is confirmed by the Board, register for and pass the Tennessee Multistate Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). Information on how to register is typically provided by NABP.

steps.title: Submit Photograph
steps.description: Upload a current, passport-style photograph as part of your LARS application, or submit it separately as directed by the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Tennessee. To work in Tennessee, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Tennessee by endorsement, create an account and submit your application through the state's Licensure and Regulatory System (LARS) portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: First-time users must register for a Person Account by selecting 'Register a Person Account' and verifying their email address. After successful registration and login, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose the 'Pharmacist Licensure by Endorsement' application.
